title: Hydriastele procera
intro: Hydriastele procera, also known as the "Kadupul tree," is a species of palm tree native to Sri Lanka and India, characterized by its large, fan-shaped leaves and towering height. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. The bioactive constituents of Hydriastele procera include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from this plant include tea, decoctions, and infusions, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues in Ayurvedic and traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Hydriastele procera is classified in the family Arecaceae, commonly known as the palm family. Within this family, Hydriastele procera belongs to the tribe Areceae. The genus Hydriastele is a group of palms native to the Pacific region, with several species identified within it. The classification of Hydriastele procera is further supported by its physical characteristics, such as its large leaf size and stem growth habit. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and diversity within the palm family.
